signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.534 — AUD dominates as the G8 leader (+0.934%) while JPY remains the primary laggard (-0.597%). The 1.531% spread reflects a strong bilateral theme supported by a +360bp RBA-BoJ carry advantage. Despite Middle East tensions and Hormuz closure headlines, the carry-positive AUD/JPY remains bid as VIX at 17.9 indicates cross-asset stress is contained below the risk-off threshold. AUD COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3%) confirms institutional backing for the move.

signals.title: USD/EUR — PPI Surprise + Fed Leadership
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1721 — The USD rally follows a hot US PPI report and the Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh as Fed Chair, signaling a hawkish continuity as Kashkari labels inflation 'too high'. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% provides a +175bp carry advantage to the USD. ECB's Lane notes oil shocks require rate hikes, but the bear steepener regime (+53.1bp 2s10s) favors USD dominance in the immediate term. EUR/USD downside targets 1.1680 if US CPI confirms the PPI beat.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5935 — COT LF net short -19,294 (18.5% long) shows specs are heavily positioned against the NZD despite a hawkish RBNZ market bias toward a hike. This positioning extreme (long% < 20%) creates significant short-squeeze risk ahead of the 27 May RBNZ meeting. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% currently provides a +150bp USD carry advantage, but a hawkish RBNZ delivery forces a crowded spec short unwind.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7263 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) shows institutional conviction while retail sentiment is 87% short. This extreme divergence indicates retail is unsuccessfully fading the institutional trend. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% provides a -60bp carry deficit for the USD, supporting the AUD bid. Retail capitulation risk rises on a sustained break above 0.7300, which would likely accelerate the move as retail shorts cover.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Correlation Break + Intervention Watch
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.821 — The correlation between USD/JPY and US 10Y yields has shifted to +0.541 (z=-2.25σ), diverging significantly from the 252d norm of +0.148. This suggests the pair is becoming hyper-sensitive to US rate pricing following the hot PPI. While carry favors USD (+300bp), 160.00 remains the critical watch level for BoJ verbal warnings or intervention. JPY COT LF net short -61,340 indicates a crowded carry trade vulnerable to any hawkish BoJ shift on 16 Jun.
